title: LAMP/RT-LAMP Reaction protocol

RT-LAMP Amplification results. 10.000 copies of synthetic SARS-Cov-2 N gene were added as the positive sample and N2 primers set [1] was used to carry the RT-LAMP reaction. Commercial enzymes reactions were carried out in NEB Isothermal Amplification Buffer (IAB) and the rest in Home-made IAB. Polymerase concentrations are indicated per uL of reaction. BstLF: home-brewed polymerase enzyme.  Bst2.0: NEB Bst2.0 Warmstart.  MMLV: M-MLV reverse transcriptase at 62,5 pg/uL reaction. RTx: NEB RTx Warmstart.  NTC: Non-Template Control. \",\"type\":\"unstyled\",\"depth\":0,\"inlineStyleRanges\":[{\"style\":\"bold\",\"offset\":0,\"length\":9}],\"entityRanges\":[],\"data\":{}}],\"entityMap\":{}}","mime":"image/png","original_name":"RT_LAMP_test_protocols.png","placeholder":"https://content.protocols.io/files/c3syhefe.jpg","source":"https://content.protocols.io/files/c3swhefe.jpg","type":1,"webp_placeholder":"https://s3.amazonaws.com/protocols-files/files/c3sxhefe.webp","webp_source":"https://content.protocols.io/files/c3svhefe.webp","width":550}},"7":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"DB0B448B749411EBA67A0A58A9FEAC2A","url":"http://paperpile.com/b/tgRxAv/XAC8"}}}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"7ehe3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"abdcg","text":"Pipettes","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4ad41","text":"Ice","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"44rk0","text":"tube grids","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f57k3","text":"0.2 uL and 1.5 uL tubes","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4k9cp","text":"Pipette tips","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"41asg","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8bcmv","text":"Equipments","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1go6g","text":"Reactions heather device: it could be from a water bath to a Real-Time machine","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"87bsa","text":"Device to register the fluorescence: from transilluminator and filters to Real-Time machine","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare 10X LAMP Primer Mix stock according to the following table: Volumes are listed to prepare 100ul of standard 10X LAMP Primer Mix stock, but you can amplify the volumes according to your requirements or modify the proportions to any other specific primer mix formulation.
Prepare stocks (100μM) of each primer in nuclease-free water and store at –20°C for up to 2 years.

Vortex and spin reaction mix. On ice If you need to add different samples; pipet 24 µL per reaction into desired reaction vessels and add 1 µL sample. Vortex and spin reaction mix. Always keep On ice to reduce spurious amplifications risk or sample degradation.

Prepare reaction mix as described in the following table: On ice, Always keep all components and reaction mix on ice. . All the protocols for the preparation of the buffers are available here: LAMP/RT-LAMP Buffer protocol by Tamara Matute

Incubate at 65 °C for 01:00:00 Time can be between 20 - 60 minutes depending on the specific assay. Specific temperature can vary according to the optimal temperature of the primer set used. The reaction can be carried out in any heater device from a water bath to real-time machines but the performance of the reactions can vary with respect to the temperature control.

Incubate at 65 °C for 01:00:00 . Time can be between 20 - 60 minutes depending on the specific assay. Specific temperature can vary accord to the optimal temperature of the primer set used. The reaction can be carried out in any heater device from a water bath to real-time machines but the performance of the reactions can vary a little accord the temperature precision control.

Vortex and spin reaction mix. On ice If you need to add different samples; pipet 24 µL per reaction into desired reaction vessels and add 1 µL sample. Vortex and spin reaction mix. Always keep On ice to reduce spurious amplifications risk or sample degradation.
creator: Tamara Matute
